channel.title: picks on Ekansh Goenka
channel.description: Recent content in picks on Ekansh Goenka

item.title: Clean Code, Horrible Performance
item.description: Makes the case that “clean code” principles actively produce slow software. Muratori benchmarks the actual cost of polymorphism, getters/setters, and small functions — and it’s not small. Changed how I think about abstractions.

item.title: Code: The Hidden Language of Computer Hardware and Software
item.description: Builds a computer from first principles — starts with flashlights and Morse code, ends with a working CPU. The best explanation of how computers actually work that I’ve ever read.

item.title: Linux
item.description: A phenomenal open-source operating system. Lean, lightweight, performant, and really well-made overall. I love it for desktop use, server use, and especially embedded systems. I also love learning from it — it’s beautiful that it’s open-source. A great piece of engineering.

item.title: Neovim
item.description: My editor. Modal editing rewires how you think about text manipulation. Once you internalize the grammar (verb + noun: diw, ci", gqap) everything else feels slow. The plugin ecosystem and Lua config make it endlessly customizable without being bloated.

item.title: Vim Motions
item.description: The language of text editing. Once you learn the grammar — verbs (delete, change, yank) combined with nouns (word, inner ", a paragraph) — you stop thinking about keystrokes and start thinking about intent. Available everywhere: Neovim, VS Code, JetBrains, even your browser.
